review.summary: The code introduces a potential security vulnerability and lacks input validation.

findings.description: The use of Jinja2 Template with user-provided input can lead to server-side template injection (SSTI) vulnerabilities.
findings.suggestion: Consider using a safer method to render templates, such as using a predefined set of templates or sanitizing the input to prevent SSTI.

findings.description: The default template string is hardcoded and may not be suitable for all use cases.
findings.suggestion: Consider allowing the default template to be configurable or provide a more descriptive default message.

findings.description: The import statements are not organized according to PEP 8 guidelines.
findings.suggestion: Organize imports into three sections: standard library imports, related third-party imports, and local application imports.

grade.rationale: The review correctly identifies the server-side template injection (SSTI) vulnerability, specifies the location in the code, and assigns an appropriate critical severity level. It provides a concrete suggestion to mitigate the vulnerability by recommending safer template rendering methods or input sanitization. The review also appropriately addresses other non-security issues without letting them overshadow the critical security finding.
